CONTENT.Club career: On 28 May 2020, Truffert signed his first professional contract with Rennes. He made his debut for the club on 19 September 2020 against Monaco. He came on as a 41st minute substitute for Faitout Maouassa, and had an assist and scored the game-winning goal in his debut.
CONTENT.External links: Adrien Truffert at the French Football Federation (in French)Adrien Truffert at the French Football Federation (archived 2020-10-26) (in French)
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 19 May 2024As of match played 25 September 2022
CONTENT.International career: Truffert was born in Belgium to French parents, and moved back to France at a young age. He made his international debut for France at the under-19 level on 9 October 2019 against England U19.On 20 September 2022, Truffert received his first call-up to the France national team for two UEFA Nations League matches, replacing the injured Lucas Digne.

SUMMARY: Adrien Lilian Maurice Truffert (born 20 November 2001) is a French professional footballer who plays as a left-back for Ligue 1 club Rennes and the France national team.
